title: Sidecar Watchdog + Degraded Mode Architecture Plan
scope: Design 3-tier resilience model: Safe Mode → Degraded Mode → Full Mode with sidecar watchdog, runtime migrations, and health-based feature gating

context_corrections.gap_2_clarified: applyMigrations() IS called at runtime in adapter.ts:194, but fire-and-forget with silent .catch() — not truly missing, just fragile
context_corrections.gap_3_resolved: migration-pg/ has 4 SQL files. migration/ directory does not exist. The '1 vs 20' claim was from stale data — no schema gap exists.

safe_mode.description: No sidecar running. .crash_lock file present. Diagnostic UI with repair actions. Sidecar spawn skipped entirely.
safe_mode.trigger: .crash_lock file exists at startup OR sidecar exhausts all restart retries OR user selects 'retry_normal_startup' action

degraded_mode.description: Sidecar running but one or more components report Degraded or Down in HealthRegistry. Non-essential features gated (LLM tools, plugins, external providers). Essential features (config, diagnostics, health endpoint) remain available.
degraded_mode.trigger: HealthRegistry reports any component as Degraded/Down after startup OR sidecar restarted successfully but with limited health after N crashes
entry_files: packages/desktop/src/main/index.ts (mode detection, call site for degraded-mode.html)

full_mode.description: All components healthy. All features enabled. This is the current default state when nothing breaks.
full_mode.trigger: All HealthRegistry components report Healthy AND sidecar health check passes

fixes.title: Sidecar Watchdog with Auto-Restart
fixes.description: Wrap spawnLocalServer with a watchdog that detects post-startup crashes and performs exponential-backoff restarts. On final failure, transition to Safe Mode or Degraded Mode depending on crash count.

changes.change: Add new export: createWatchdog(options) → { listener, health, onCrash }
changes.before: spawnLocalServer() returns { listener: SidecarListener, health: { wait } } — no crash awareness after startup
changes.after: New function createWatchdog wraps spawnLocalServer with: (a) exit watch on returned exit promise, (b) exponential backoff restart (1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, max 30s), (c) max 5 retries, (d) onCrash callback for mode transition orchestration, (e) crash count exposed for caller decision-making. Does NOT alter spawnLocalServer signature — creates new function alongside it.
changes.risk_detail: Exponential backoff in test environment must be mockable. Crash lock file (.crash_lock) must be removed on successful restart. Restart must create fresh utilityProcess — cannot reuse dead child process.

changes.change: Replace spawnLocalServer call with createWatchdog; add onCrash handler that transitions to Safe Mode or Degraded Mode based on crash count and HealthRegistry status
changes.before: const { listener, health } = yield* Effect.promise(() => spawnLocalServer(...))
changes.after: const { listener, health } = yield* Effect.promise(() => createWatchdog({ spawnArgs: [...], onCrash: (count, status) => { if (count >= 5) setInitStep({ phase: 'safe_mode', error: ... }); else setInitStep({ phase: 'degraded_mode' }); } }))
changes.risk_detail: onCrash callback runs on utilityProcess exit event — must not throw or it'll trigger unhandledRejection → process.exit(1)

risks: Exponential backoff accumulation across multiple sessions — need reset-on-success or per-session max
utilityProcess may leak if kill() fails during restart sequence
Race: restart in progress while user clicks 'kill sidecar' from Safe Mode
onCrash callback must be defensive — any throw causes process.exit(1) via unhandledRejection handler
validation: Unit test: mock utilityProcess to simulate crash after 2s, verify restart with backoff timing
Unit test: simulate 5 consecutive crashes, verify onCrash called with count=5
Integration test: launch sidecar, kill process, verify watchdog restarts within backoff window
Smoke test: verify .crash_lock is absent after successful restart

fixes.title: Robust Migration Application at Runtime
fixes.description: Make applyMigrations() blocking during adapter construction so schema is guaranteed before any query runs. Report migration outcome to HealthRegistry. Remove the fire-and-forget .catch() that silently swallows failures.

changes.change: Convert synchronous init+fire-and-forget-migrate into async init with awaited migration, then report to HealthRegistry
changes.risk_detail: Making makePgAdapter async changes its return type. All callers must be updated. This may propagate through Effect layers — makeLocalPgAdapter() and makePgAdapter() both return Interface synchronously today.

downstream_consumers: Callers of makePgAdapter() and makeLocalPgAdapter() — checked: these are used in Effect Layer construction which can handle async
type_changes: makePgAdapter: returns Interface → returns Promise<Interface> (potentially, or stays sync with try/catch)

risks: If migration fails and throws, the entire sidecar startup fails — this is intentional (fail-fast) but must be caught by server bootstrap to transition to Safe Mode rather than crash-looping
Migration takes time — could extend startup beyond current 60s timeout. Consider increasing SIDECAR_START_STALL_TIMEOUT or reporting migration progress
DatabaseInitError thrown during Effect Layer construction — must be caught by Layer lifecycle, not crash the process
validation: Unit test: call makePgAdapter with corrupt DB, verify DatabaseInitError thrown with migration cause
Integration test: verify HealthRegistry shows 'pglite' component as Healthy after successful migration
Smoke test: delete DB files, restart app, verify migrations run and app starts normally

fixes.title: Health-Based Feature Gating (SystemHealth Service)
fixes.description: New SystemHealth Effect service that aggregates HealthRegistry component statuses into a system-level mode (Full/Degraded/Down). Feature gating middleware that queries SystemHealth before enabling LLM, tools, plugins, external providers.

changes.change: Add SystemHealth service and layer on top of HealthRegistry. Exports: getMode(): Effect<SystemMode>, isHealthy(): Effect<boolean>, isDegraded(): Effect<boolean>. Mode algorithm: if any component Down → Safe; if any Degraded → Degraded; else Full.
changes.before: Only HealthRegistry with per-component get/set/getAll. No system-level aggregation.
changes.after: Add SystemMode enum: Full | Degraded | Down. Add SystemHealth service with getMode(), getAllComponents(), aggregateHealth(). New layer: SystemHealth.layer = HealthRegistry.layer.pipe(Layer.map(...)).

changes.change: Extend /global/health endpoint to include system mode and degraded components list
changes.before: return { healthy: true as const, version: InstallationVersion, components }
changes.risk_detail: Backward-compatible: client-side ServerHealth type { healthy: boolean; version?: string } still works — just add optional fields

changes.change: Add feature gating: before enabling LLM routing, tool execution, plugin loading — check SystemHealth mode. If Degraded, gate non-essential features. If Safe, refuse all operations except health/config.
changes.before: No feature gating — all routes and services load unconditionally.
changes.after: New middleware or effect combinator: requireMode(minMode) that short-circuits with a degraded-mode error response when mode < minMode.
changes.risk_detail: Must not block health endpoint, config endpoints, or diagnostics endpoints. Only gate LLM calls, tool execution, plugin hooks, MCP server connections.

downstream_consumers: Every route that calls LLM, tools, or plugins
packages/app/src/utils/server-health.ts (needs to handle optional mode field)

risks: Circular dependency: SystemHealth depends on HealthRegistry; HealthRegistry layer might depend on code that needs SystemHealth
Feature gating too aggressive — what if only DuckDB is down but PGlite is healthy? LLM should still work.
SolidJS reactivity: mode changes at runtime need to propagate to UI without page reload
validation: Unit test: HealthRegistry set 'pglite' to Degraded, verify SystemHealth.getMode() returns Degraded
Unit test: HealthRegistry set 'pglite' to Down, verify SystemHealth.getMode() returns Down
Integration test: start server with unhealthy component, verify /global/health returns mode=Degraded
Smoke test: kill PGlite connection, verify LLM calls are rejected with degraded mode error

fixes.title: Wire Degraded Mode into Desktop Startup Flow
fixes.description: Emit the existing { phase: 'degraded_mode' } InitStep when sidecar starts but components are unhealthy. Add degraded-mode.html renderer entry point. Add Degraded Mode IPC actions (similar to SafeModeAction).

changes.change: After sidecar health check passes, query /global/health for component statuses. If any Degraded/Down, emit { phase: 'degraded_mode' } and load degraded-mode.html instead of normal app.
changes.before: SafeMode only on crash_lock detection. No degraded path exists.
changes.after: After successful health check: fetch /global/health, inspect mode field. If mode !== 'Full', setInitStep({ phase: 'degraded_mode' }) and create dedicated window.

changes.change: Add DegradedModeAction type (subset of SafeModeAction: 'export_debug_logs', 'open_logs', 'disable_plugins', 'disable_mcp', 'retry_normal_startup'). Extend InitStep degraded_mode with component details.
changes.before: InitStep includes { phase: 'degraded_mode' } but unused. SafeModeAction has 9 actions.
changes.after: DegradedModeAction type with 5 actions. InitStep.degraded_mode extends with: { phase: 'degraded_mode', components: Record<string, ComponentHealth>, mode: SystemMode }

changes.change: Create degraded mode UI: Shows which components are unhealthy, which features are limited, allows diagnostic export and plugin/MCP disable. Cleaner than Safe Mode — no crash lock, just limited functionality.
changes.after: SolidJS component similar to safe-mode.tsx pattern but with: (a) component health table, (b) limited functionality notice, (c) action buttons: export logs, disable plugins/MCP, retry full startup. Uses SafeModeCard component.
changes.risk_detail: New HTML entry point must be registered in Electron build (vite config, BrowserWindow loadURL path)

affected_files: packages/desktop/esbuild.config.mjs (or vite config — new entry point)
downstream_consumers: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-contract.ts (add IPC channels to contract type)

risks: New degraded-mode.html must be in Electron build output — vite/esbuild config must include it
Degraded mode window must not conflict with loading overlay or main window creation
IPC channel additions must match between contract type, channel constants, and handler registration
validation: Unit test: verify setInitStep({ phase: 'degraded_mode' }) is emitted when /global/health returns mode=Degraded
Integration test: start sidecar with unhealthy component, verify degraded mode window loads
Smoke test: simulate PGlite down, verify degraded UI shows component health table with 'pglite: Down'

apply_order_rationale: B first (migration is prerequisite for database health signals), then C (SystemHealth service needed by D and A), then D (mode UI depends on health signals), then A last (watchdog is most complex and depends on mode transition infrastructure from C/D).
rollback_plan: Revert each fix in reverse order (A → D → C → B). Fix-A is purely additive — removing the watchdog wrapper and falling back to spawnLocalServer directly restores original behavior. Fix-D has new files that must be deleted on rollback.
out_of_scope: Live health monitoring dashboard in renderer (future enhancement)
Automatic repair actions (e.g., auto-rebuild corrupt DB) — Safe Mode actions today are user-initiated
Persistent degraded mode across app restarts — each launch re-evaluates health fresh
Cross-process health propagation (renderer ↔ main ↔ sidecar) beyond existing IPC channels
